narrative.headline: 2018 Kia Forte: engine complaints dominate, but air bags carry the safety risk
narrative.summary: Across 134 owner complaints to NHTSA, engine problems lead by a wide margin at 57 reports (43%). The next areas are far smaller: electrical system and air bags tie at 16 reports each (12%). Air bags stand out for safety: all 8 crashes and all 8 injuries in this data sit in that category, with owners reporting air bags that did not deploy. The engine category includes 1 fire and 2 crashes.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: By far the most-reported area: 57 complaints (43%), including 1 fire and 2 crashes. No injuries reported in this category.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 16 complaints (12%), including 1 fire and 2 crashes. One owner reports the trunk popping open on its own, even while parked or driving.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: The biggest safety concern: 16 complaints (12%) but tied to 8 crashes and 8 injuries, every crash and injury in this data. Owners report air bags that failed to deploy in a collision.

issues.blurb: 10 complaints (7%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 8 complaints (6%), including 1 crash. One owner reports a rough idle at a standstill early in the car's life.
